reasoning: The FRED statistical trend analysis yields a combined estimate of P(increase) = 0.71 for early March, noting an upward linear drift and historical seasonal behavior that typically pushes rates slightly higher in early March. A web search reveals that the CME FedWatch tool implies a 96% probability that the Fed will hold rates steady in March 2026, meaning a Fed rate cut is highly unlikely to overwhelm this minor upward drift. Therefore, the probability of an increase by March 8 is anchored closely to the FRED 0.71 estimate. The likelihood decreases toward 0.5 for the later resolution date (March 31) due to increased forecast uncertainty, the possibility that later economic data shifts expectations toward rate cuts heading into Q2, and increased variance in predicting standard noise a month out.
